objective: Enumerate privilege escalation vectors from svc-deploy to root. Focus on SSH CA cert signing, deployers group permissions, and deployment infrastructure.

current_access.notes: Stable SSH session, full TTY. Service account for deployment automation. Issues SSH certificates per platform activity logs.

in_scope: Investigate /opt/principal/ssh/ — SSH CA configuration, key material, cert signing capability
Investigate /opt/principal/ — deployment scripts, configs, writable paths
Enumerate running processes and their owners (especially Jetty)
Check H2 database for credentials or writable access
Enumerate other users' home directories for readable files
out_of_scope: Exploitation, modifying files, writing payloads, SSH cert forging, brute-forcing. Enumerate and report only.
allowed_actions: Read SSH CA config and key permissions
completion_criteria: All enumeration vectors checked and documented
Privesc leads ranked by confidence and feasibility
return_conditions: Critical finding that requires immediate Oracle decision
notes:
PRIORITY VECTORS:
1. SSH CA at /opt/principal/ssh/ — svc-deploy issues SSH certs per platform logs. If svc-deploy can sign certs with the CA key, we can forge a cert for root. Check if CA private key is readable by svc-deploy or deployers group.
2. deployers group (gid=1001) — check what files/dirs this group owns or can write to. Deployment scripts may run as root.
3. Cron jobs — deployment automation likely has scheduled tasks.
4. H2 database — embedded DB may be at /opt/principal/ with additional creds.

KNOWN USERS: admin, svc-deploy, jthompson, amorales, bwright, kkumar, mwilson, lzhang
Platform version: v1.2.0, Java 21.0.10, Jetty 12.x
